description_long: This object is a robust, polygonal stone block with a broad, flat top surface and four protruding, angular extensions at its base, giving it a fortified appearance. The block is constructed from a single, solid piece of stone, with beveled edges and a slightly raised central platform. Its geometric design suggests it is intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle, possibly to prevent vehicle or pedestrian passage. The surface is matte and appears unpolished, with no visible signs of weathering or decorative markings. The overall form is symmetrical, and the extensions at the base provide additional stability, making it difficult to move. Its utilitarian design is well-suited for urban environments where durable, immovable barriers are required.
description: A solid, polygonal stone block with a flat top and four angular base extensions, designed as a sturdy urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central flat top and the prominent angular extension facing forward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the right-side extension and the symmetry of the base protrusions.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back extension and the overall polygonal shape of the block.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the left-side extension most visible, mirroring the right side.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a protective barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surface appears clean and unworn
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely placed individually as a standalone barrier
